title: Trump Announces 'Project Freedom' to Escort Ships Out of Strait of Hormuz Starting Monday
summary: US President Donald Trump announced 'Project Freedom,' a US-led initiative to escort foreign ships trapped in the Strait of Hormuz starting Monday. The operation targets vessels from neutral countries stranded by Iran's blockade, with Trump warning that interference will face forceful response.
contents:
## Project Freedom: US to Escort Ships Through Strait of Hormuz

### Announcement
US President Donald Trump declared on May 3 that the United States would begin escorting foreign ships out of the Strait of Hormuz under an operation dubbed 'Project Freedom,' scheduled to commence Monday morning Middle East time.

### Key Details
- Trump described the initiative as a 'humanitarian gesture' for neutral countries whose vessels remain trapped in the strategic waterway
- The president noted many ships face dwindling food and sanitary supplies for their crews
- Trump warned that any interference with the escort operation 'will, unfortunately, face forceful consequences'
- The operation targets vessels from countries 'not involved in the Middle Eastern dispute'

### Strategic Context
The announcement arrived amid a fragile three-week ceasefire between the US and Iran. Iran effectively closed the strait after the US-Israel war on Iran began February 28. Tehran later offered some ships passage via routes closer to its shore, sometimes charging fees.

The US imposed a naval blockade on Iranian ports since April 13, cutting Tehran's oil revenue. Iran's army spokesman Mohammad Akraminia declared the same day that no vessel — friendly or hostile — may transit without Iranian authorization.

### Diplomatic Tensions
Trump simultaneously rejected Iran's 14-point peace proposal as 'not acceptable,' while acknowledging 'very positive discussions' between US and Iranian representatives continue. The IRGC stated it remains 'fully prepared' to counter any US 'adventures or foolishness.'

### Risk Assessment
Analysts warn the escort mission brings US forces closer to Iranian coastal defenses, potentially shattering the ceasefire. The US military previously stated it lacks readiness to accompany vessels through the narrow waterway under fire from Iranian territory.
